near_leaf_pairs.note: Same underlying call: the infographic's 'opportunities across bond markets' / 'opportunities in public fixed income, with attractive yields and robust liquidity' is the only bond-positive text in the PDF; model booked it at the broad FI leaf, GT at high-quality govt bonds. Overlapping leaves, views agree.

Both derive from the quality-tilt-in-credit stance; leaves are in the same Fixed Income - Investment Grade category. Views diverge: the PDF only says 'Tilt up in quality, using rigorous bottom-up credit selection' which supports the model's O on quality issuers; GT's N rests on tight-spreads/selectivity reasoning (Middle East repricing detail) that appears nowhere in the 2-page PDF. On this source, the model reading is better grounded; GT's N is an inference from material outside the PDF.

model_only_judgments.reason: Explicit in source: 'Explore private credit investing beyond direct lending, including more resilient areas like asset-based finance and real estate debt' (p.1 grid). GT captured asset-based finance (row 74) but omitted the co-listed real estate debt.

Explicit in source: 'We see sustainable opportunities in AI infrastructure lending when pricing is attractive and lender covenants are strong' (p.1 grid). Conditional phrasing makes 90 confidence a touch high, but the O call itself is well supported and GT simply did not enumerate it.

gt_only_root_causes.note: PDF's only hook is 'opportunities across bond markets'; the duration-overweight/safe-haven/curve detail in GT commentary is absent from the 2-page PDF and cannot be derived from it.
MBS is never mentioned in the PDF (only in boilerplate risk disclosures). GT commentary appears authored from the full Cyclical Outlook article, not this infographic.
'High quality securitised credit' language does not appear in the PDF; nearest source text ('asset-based finance') was already matched to PD - Asset-Based. GT sourced from beyond the infographic.
Household equity-ownership/rebalancing argument is entirely absent from the PDF; equities are not mentioned at all in the infographic.
No EM content anywhere in the PDF; GT commentary (EM central banks, real yield buffers) comes from the full article.
No BoE/ECB or Europe-specific content in the PDF; GT commentary drawn from outside this source.

PDF localizes concern to corporate direct lending (which the model emitted as PD - Direct Lending U, matched) and explicitly recommends exploring private credit beyond direct lending — a broad asset-class U partially conflicts with the source; GT's GFC-parallel reasoning is not in the PDF.
Derivable only as the inverse of 'tilt up in quality'; the spillover-to-public-HY and covenant detail in GT commentary is absent from the PDF.

notes: The source PDF is a 2-page infographic whose only substantive content is the page-1 investment-implications grid (page 2 is disclosures); the model enumerated all 9 grid implications and matched or near-matched every GT row that is actually grounded in that grid. Eight of ten gt_only rows cite reasoning (Middle East conflict repricing, GFC mortgage parallels, BoE/ECB, agency MBS) that does not exist anywhere in the PDF, indicating the ground truth was authored from the full Cyclical Outlook web article rather than this document — consistent with the pilot-04 disputability note. The single view divergence (quality credit O vs IG N) is a GT-side inference dispute, not a model reading error, on this source.
